name: REI Co-op® Mastercard®
effective_cost: Free — no annual fee, but requires an active REI Co-op membership ($30 one-time fee)

verdict.text: Solid if you're already an REI regular — 5% at REI plus a real $50 experiences credit — but the 1.5% base rate is unremarkable and it only exists for Co-op members

earn_rates.category: REI retail stores & REI.com (net purchases, after returns/credits)
REI purchases during the annual Cardmember Double Rewards event

earn_note: Rewards post as they're earned and never expire for the life of the account — better than most store cards' certificate-and-clock model. The 1.5% base rate is fine but unexceptional; this card earns its keep almost entirely at REI itself. An active REI Co-op membership ($30 one-time fee, separate from the card) is required to hold the card at all.

redemption_options.method: Redeemed at REI (in-store or online) as they're earned — no minimum threshold, no expiration

points.per_100k: $1,500 (at the 1.5% base rate on non-REI spend)
points.note: Functions like cash back but is spent at REI rather than deposited or applied as a statement credit — check REI's own redemption flow for whether non-REI cash-out is offered before assuming full flexibility.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners — this is a retail rewards currency tied to REI, not a travel points program.

credits.name: REI Co-op Experiences Credit
credits.subtitle: $50/yr toward REI classes
credits.description: A $50 statement credit, once per calendar year, when you book an REI Co-op Experience (classes/events) with the card. Excludes REI gear rentals. Credit posts 6–8 weeks after purchase.
tips: Only useful if you actually take an REI class or guided outing each year — it doesn't apply to rentals or gear.
One credit per calendar year, so time a $50+ Experience purchase deliberately rather than letting it go unused.

insurance.detail: Up to $250/claim · max 4 claims per 12 months · 120-day window vs. a lower advertised price
Doubles manufacturer warranties of ≤24 months, up to an additional 24 months · max $10,000/claim
Up to $1,500/trip · $5,000/account per 12 months · secondary coverage
Up to $1,500/incident · max 2 claims per 12 months · worldwide
Secondary coverage · up to 31 consecutive days · must decline the counter's own CDW

protection_note: A genuinely solid World Elite Mastercard insurance suite for a no-fee card — extended warranty in particular ($10,000/claim, doubles up to 2 years) beats most no-fee cash-back cards outright. Trip cancellation and luggage coverage are both secondary and capped modestly, so don't rely on this alone for an expensive international trip.
rental_note: Secondary CDW via Mastercard's MasterRental program — decline the rental counter's own waiver, pay for the entire rental with this card, and it covers physical damage/theft up to 31 days. Pays after your personal auto policy, and excludes personal injury/liability.

status_perks.name: LoungeKey airport lounge access
status_perks.note: Not complimentary — cardholders get LoungeKey membership with pay-as-you-go visits at $32/person, across 1,000+ lounges in 120+ countries. A minor perk, not free lounge access.

services.name: REI Co-op membership required
services.detail: The card is only available to REI Co-op members, which requires a separate one-time $30 lifetime membership fee — not included in or waived by the card.

services.name: $100 REI gift card welcome bonus
services.detail: Earned after one purchase made anywhere outside of REI within 60 days of account opening.

services.name: Mastercard ID Theft Protection
services.detail: Dark/deep web monitoring and identity-theft resolution services; requires separate enrollment.

services.name: World Elite Mastercard Concierge
services.detail: 24/7 referral line for travel, dining, and event bookings — you pay for anything arranged through it.

services.name: Travel & Emergency Assistance
services.detail: Referral-only help (medical, legal, translation, lost ticket/luggage locator) while traveling; costs of any actual services are your responsibility.

services.name: Roadside assistance
services.detail: Emergency towing/lockout/jump-start dispatch; service fees are billed to your account, not free.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: 0% on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: $2M annual donation to REI Cooperative Action Fund
services.detail: Capital One commits an annual donation funded by the card program — a brand-level perk, not a personal cardholder benefit.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Earns rewards on their spend (into your account)
Covered by the same World Elite Mastercard purchase/warranty protections

additional_cards.note: Free to add, and authorized users share the card's above-average purchase protection and extended warranty coverage — a real benefit given this card's insurance suite is stronger than most no-fee cards.

timeline.text: REI Co-op moved its cobrand card program from U.S. Bank to Capital One, relaunching as the REI Co-op Mastercard with a World Elite Mastercard tier and the current 5%/1.5% earning structure.
